compiled_state.author: reconciler
compiled_state.description:
Add a first-class ticket status `idea` for future work that is captured but not yet
designed or ready for implementation. It maps to a Jira status `IDEA` and is kept out
of the work-dispatch and store-health hot paths so a noisy, high-volume backlog of
ideas never taxes or pollutes the day-to-day parallel-agent workflow.

## Context

Teams need a durable parking lot for rough, undesigned future work — separate from
`open` (which means "designed enough to work; eligible for `ready`/`next-batch`").
Today the only pre-work status is `open`, so a captured idea immediately becomes
claimable work. `idea` fills that gap. It is a *status* (any ticket type can hold it),
reached by the same free transitions as every other status — rebar gives clients tools
to follow their own workflow, not a rigid state machine. The status is carved out of the
automated dispatch surfaces (`ready`, `next-batch`) and the completion-attestation +
store-health machinery wherever its undesigned looseness would generate noise, while
remaining fully listable and searchable so ideas can be found and promoted later.

The design was settled in a brainstorm and is decomposed into five child stories, each a
coherent single-session outcome with its own acceptance criteria and file-impact:

- Status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion (types, transition validator, list filter,
  ready/next-batch exclusion).
- `idea → closed` gate bypass (the four completion/attestation close gates; keep the
  structural open-children guard).
- `validate` noise exemptions (empty-epic/orphan/missing-desc/contract/count exempt;
  structural checks retained).
- Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping (five status maps + parity test).
- Atomic `idea` command across CLI/MCP/library (single genesis CREATE, type `epic`).

Out of scope (tracked as a separate `discovered_from` follow-up): the reconciler
transition-failure isolation bug — a failed Jira transition should apply every other
mutation in the pass and report the failure at the END rather than aborting mid-pass.

## Success Criteria

- An idea can be captured in one atomic operation on any surface and is never momentarily
  claimable.
- `idea` tickets never appear in `ready` or `next-batch`, and never contribute
  empty-epic/orphan/missing-description/contract/count findings to the `validate` score,
  while still appearing in `list` and `search`.
- Rejecting an idea (`idea → closed`) succeeds with no completion/attestation gate, while
  the structural open-children guard still holds.
- `idea` round-trips to Jira `IDEA` (subject to the documented workflow-transition
  prerequisite), with all status maps consistent and parity-tested.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Child story "status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion" is closed and certified.
- [ ] Child story "`idea → closed` gate bypass" is closed and certified.
- [ ] Child story "`validate` noise exemptions" is closed and certified.
- [ ] Child story "Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping" is closed and certified.
- [ ] Child story "atomic `idea` command (CLI/MCP/library)" is closed and certified.
- [ ] The committed `CLAUDE.md` documents the `idea` status and its lifecycle.
- [ ] `make lint` and the full test suite pass on the integrated result.
compiled_state.title: Provide a way to distinguish between rough ideas and ideas that have been fully planned and are ready to implement.
